ALL SPECIALTIES / RESPIRATORY THERAPIST / LOUISIANA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Louisiana Respiratory Therapist License

Louisiana Respiratory Therapist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Respiratory Therapist applications in Louisiana run through Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners — RT. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 8 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

  1. STEP 1

    Create an Account on My LSBME Portal

    Visit the My LSBME online portal (online.lsbme.la.gov), click 'Register as a New Applicant,' and follow the instructions to create your applicant account and log in.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Initiate New Respiratory Therapist Application

    From your My LSBME account dashboard, select 'Apply for a New License' and choose 'Respiratory Therapist' from the available professions.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Complete Online Application Form

    Fill out all required sections of the online application within the My LSBME portal, providing accurate details regarding your personal information, education, clinical experience, and answering all background questions truthfully.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Submit Supporting Documents

    Upload all necessary documentation, including official transcripts, National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) credentials, and any other required forms directly through your My LSBME account.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fee

    Complete the payment for the non-refundable application fee via credit card through the My LSBME portal to formally submit your application.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Monitor Application Status

    Regularly check the status of your application within your My LSBME account and promptly respond to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners.

    Fee: $100 · Timeline: 4 to 8 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$100
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$100

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners — RT
Phone
504-568-6820

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Respiratory Therapist renewal in Louisiana follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annual
Renewal fee
$50
Continuing education
24 hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Louisiana respiratory therapist license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Louisiana have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?

No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Louisiana. To work in Louisiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Louisiana?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$100

Processing time

4 to 8 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) entry-level examination

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Annual · $50

Licensing board

Louisiana State Board of Medical Examiners — RT

lsbme.la.gov/

504-568-6820

This page is maintained as a reference for respiratory therapists and recruiters evaluating Louisiana assignments.
